title: Job Openings at Consulting DCC
content:
Consulting DCC is a professional consulting firm that provides strategic, operational, recruitment, and advisory services designed to support business growth and improve organizational efficiency. We work closely with clients to understand their unique needs, address operational challenges, and deliver practical, results-driven solutions. Our approach is rooted in professionalism, integrity, and a strong understanding of business processes, enabling us to help organizations strengthen their structures, attract and retain the right talent, make informed decisions, and achieve sustainable performance.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Procurement Officer
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Process purchase requisitions from production, stores, and admin departments.
	Source and evaluate reliable suppliers for ingredients, packaging, and operational items.
	Prepare accurate purchase orders in line with company procedures.
	Liaise with suppliers on orders, deliveries, and pricing agreements.
	Monitor and negotiate costs to achieve budgeted savings.
	Track deliveries to ensure on-time arrival of materials.
	Verify quality of received materials before acceptance.
	Collaborate with inventory teams to prevent stock-outs.
	Maintain accurate procurement documents and records.
	Conduct monthly market price research for key materials.
	Ensure compliance with procurement policies.
	Generate monthly procurement and supplier performance reports.


Requirements
Education:


	Bachelor’s Degree or HND in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Accounting, or related field.


Experience:


	2–3 years of experience in procurement or supply chain.


Salary
N250,000 per month.

Application Closing Date: 3rd April, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: career@consultingdcc.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Outlet Manager

Job Locations: Ikorodu, Opebi, Surulere, Gbagada, Ogudu, Lekki, Ado-Road, & Ajah, Lagos

Job Responsibilities


	Drive sales growth and ensure daily revenue targets are achieved.
	Manage, supervise, and motivate staff for optimal performance.
	Oversee inventory control, stock levels, and loss prevention.
	Ensure smooth daily operations and excellent customer service.
	Reporting, and compliance with company standards.


Requirements


	Interested candidates should possess relevant qualifications with years of work experience.


Key skills:


	Strong sales and target-driven mindset
	Excellent people management and leadership skills
	Inventory and stock management skills
	Good communication and customer service skills
	Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced QSR environment.


Salary
N150,000 - N200,000 / month.

Application Closing Date: 12th March, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: career@consultingdcc.com.ng using "Outlet Magaer- Gbagada" as the subject of the email.

 

 

3.) Area Manager (Multi-Store Operations)

Job Locations: Festac, Ikotun, Ogudu, Ikorodu, and Ikeja - Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking an experienced and results-driven Area Manager to oversee and manage multiple outlets, ensure operational excellence, and drive sales performance across assigned locations.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee daily operations of multiple stores within assigned area
	Drive sales growth and ensure outlets meet revenue targets
	Supervise, coach, and evaluate Outlet Managers and store teams
	Ensure compliance with company standards, policies, and procedures
	Monitor inventory control, cost management, and loss prevention
	Analyze performance reports and implement improvement strategies
	Handle escalated customer and operational issues.


Requirements


	HND / BSc in Business Administration or related field
	3 - 5 proven of experience managing multiple outlets (QSR or retail preferred)
	Strong leadership, people management, and analytical skills
	Ability to work in a fast-paced, target-driven environment.


Salary
N250,000 - N270,000 per month.

Application Closing Date: 15th March, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: career@consultingdcc.com.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

     

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Quality Assurance Executive",
  "description": "Zamiva Transnational Services - Our client, a leading Pharmaceutical Manufacturing&nbsp;company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Assurance Executive

Job Location:&nbsp;Mowe, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job&nbsp;Description


	A&nbsp;Quality Assurance Officer (QA) ensures products or services meet established quality, safety, and regulatory standards (e.g., ISO, GMP) by monitoring processes, performing audits, and implementing corrective actions.
	Key duties include inspecting, reviewing documentation, testing, and driving continuous improvement to minimize defects.


Key Responsibilities &amp; Duties


	Compliance &amp; Auditing:&nbsp;Develop and implement QA policies, procedures, and standards to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.
	Inspections &amp; Testing:&nbsp;Perform visual inspections, sampling, and tests on products or materials to verify quality.
	Documentation &amp; Reporting:&nbsp;Review and approve standard operating procedures (SOPs), batch records, and quality reports.
	Corrective Actions (CAPA):&nbsp;Identify issues, conduct root cause analysis, and implement Corrective and Preventive Actions.
	Process Improvement:&nbsp;Monitor operational processes, analyze data, and provide recommendations to enhance efficiency and quality.
	Training &amp; Support:&nbsp;Train staff on quality standards and foster a culture of quality awareness.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Biochemistry, Microbiology , Laboratory Scienece, etc
	Certifications in Quality Assurance or Auditing are often preferred.
	2-3 years of experience in a Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Company (Candidates without Quality Assurance experience in the pharmaceutical industry should not apply.)


Required Skills and Qualifications:


	Technical Knowledge:&nbsp;In-depth knowledge of QA processes, industry-specific standards (e.g., GMP), and compliance requirements.
	Analytical Skills:&nbsp;Strong ability to analyze data, identify trends, and solve problems.
	Attention to Detail:&nbsp;Meticulous approach to reviewing documentation and inspecting products.
	Communication:&nbsp;Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Experience:&nbsp;Previous experience in quality assurance, quality control, or a similar role.


Salary
N400,000 (Monthly gross).

Application Closing Date
20th March, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their detailed CV to:&nbsp;transnationalzamiva21@yahoo.com&nbsp;or&nbsp;metusmetus73@gmail.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	For more information, WhatsAap to: 09060194404
	(Preference will be given to candidates living in close proximity to MOWE, Ikeja, Isheri, Ojodu-Berger, Alausa, Ojota, Ketu, Magodo,Omole, Magboro and environs).
	Willingness to relocate to Mowe, Ogun Stateat the candidate&#39;s expense is also welcome.

Quality Assurance Executive Job at Zamiva Transnational Services




        
    

        
        Zamiva Transnational Services - Our client, a leading Pharmaceutical Manufacturing company, is recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Quality Assurance Executive

Job Location: Mowe, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	A Quality Assurance Officer (QA) ensures products or services meet established quality, safety, and regulatory standards (e.g., ISO, GMP) by monitoring processes, performing audits, and implementing corrective actions.
	Key duties include inspecting, reviewing documentation, testing, and driving continuous improvement to minimize defects.


Key Responsibilities & Duties


	Compliance & Auditing: Develop and implement QA policies, procedures, and standards to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements.
	Inspections & Testing: Perform visual inspections, sampling, and tests on products or materials to verify quality.
	Documentation & Reporting: Review and approve standard operating procedures (SOPs), batch records, and quality reports.
	Corrective Actions (CAPA): Identify issues, conduct root cause analysis, and implement Corrective and Preventive Actions.
	Process Improvement: Monitor operational processes, analyze data, and provide recommendations to enhance efficiency and quality.
	Training & Support: Train staff on quality standards and foster a culture of quality awareness.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Biochemistry, Microbiology , Laboratory Scienece, etc
	Certifications in Quality Assurance or Auditing are often preferred.
	2-3 years of experience in a Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Company (Candidates without Quality Assurance experience in the pharmaceutical industry should not apply.)


Required Skills and Qualifications:


	Technical Knowledge: In-depth knowledge of QA processes, industry-specific standards (e.g., GMP), and compliance requirements.
	Analytical Skills: Strong ability to analyze data, identify trends, and solve problems.
	Attention to Detail: Meticulous approach to reviewing documentation and inspecting products.
	Communication: Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Experience: Previous experience in quality assurance, quality control, or a similar role.


Salary
N400,000 (Monthly gross).

Application Closing Date
20th March, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their detailed CV to: transnationalzamiva21@yahoo.com or metusmetus73@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	For more information, WhatsAap to: 09060194404
	(Preference will be given to candidates living in close proximity to MOWE, Ikeja, Isheri, Ojodu-Berger, Alausa, Ojota, Ketu, Magodo,Omole, Magboro and environs).
	Willingness to relocate to Mowe, Ogun Stateat the candidate's expense is also welcome.
    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

We are seasoned human resources professionals and management strategists with enviable corporate exposure and experience spanning various sectors of the economy, including banking, logistics &amp; courier, engineering, manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, food &amp; beverages, oil &amp; gas, telecommunication, private security services, hospitality.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant Manager, Pharmaceutical Procurement

Job Location:&nbsp;Mowe, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities&nbsp;


	Responsible for overseeing all purchasing functions.
	Leads and implements the procurement of materials, components, equipment, and services at favorable pricing.
	Ensures suppliers are consistent with scheduled deliveries and maintain specifications for applicable materials/equipment.
	Lead supplier selection and pre-qualification process.
	Investigates and recommends sources of supply and purchases through contractual arrangements to affect cost savings.
	Obtains sources, quotes, generates, and processes requisitions, and maintains related records, logs, files, artwork as pertained to the purchasing department.
	Check all purchasing invoices for accuracy in billing.&nbsp;
	Presents vendor cost comparison reports to upper management as requested. &bull; Monitor and forecast upcoming levels of demand and track inventory levels.
	Other assigned tasks as necessary assigned by Management.&nbsp;
	Responsible for following all safety regulations and complying with SOP&#39;s, EPA, OSHA, FDA, and cGMP&#39;s while performing duties.
	Maintain cleanliness and appearance of work area.&nbsp;
	Well versed in analysis of spend data and cost models for continuous improvement.
	Maintain relationships with various functional groups across the organization including Manufacturing, Quality, Distribution, and Administration.&nbsp;
	Other duties as assigned.

	BSC&nbsp;/ HND in any relevant field. However, a practical pharmaceutical manufacturing procurement experience is non-negotiable.
	5 - 6 years of procurement experience in the pharmaceutical industry Only( CANDIDATES WITHOUT PROCUREMENT PHARMACEUTICAL EXPERIENCE SHOULD NOT APPLY).
	Extensive knowledge of cGMP, FDA, and other pharmaceutical requirements.
	Experience in sourcing both pharmaceuticals, medical devices, and equipment.
	Experience of pharmaceutical supply chain systems in developing contexts and logistics.
	Successful experience in audit/assessments and the implementation of supply chain management improvements.
	Ability to identify trends within pharmaceuticals industry.&nbsp;
	Ability to establish supplier specifications, measure conformance, and identify process improvement opportunities.&nbsp;
	Knowledge of risk management, with the ability to identify and address anticipated issues. &bull; Ability to work independently and troubleshoot challenges.&nbsp;
	Must possess strong verbal, written, and oral communication skills.
	Must be able to work independently and with a team.
	Must possess problem-solving skills.&nbsp;
	Recognize priorities and take action tomake productive use of time.

N400,000 &ndash; N500,000 / month.

Assistant Manager, Pharmaceutical Procurement Job at Zamiva Transnational Services




        
    

        
        Zamiva Transnational Services is a full-fledged human capital development and management consultancy servicing company incorporated in August, 2021 with its corporate Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to ensuring quality value addition to our clients’ businesses spanning all sectors of the economy in the areas of HR consulting, outsourcing, talent acquisition, executive search, employee/reference background verification, payroll management, learning & development, renting, catering and janitorial services, etc., leveraging research, technology, a highly skilled and passionate human capital base, and our core values of Collaboration, Leadership, Integrity, Continuous Improvement, High Performance and Expertise.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are seasoned human resources professionals and management strategists with enviable corporate exposure and experience spanning various sectors of the economy, including banking, logistics & courier, engineering, manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, food & beverages, oil & gas, telecommunication, private security services, hospitality.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant Manager, Pharmaceutical Procurement

Job Location: Mowe, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities 


	Responsible for overseeing all purchasing functions.
	Leads and implements the procurement of materials, components, equipment, and services at favorable pricing.
	Ensures suppliers are consistent with scheduled deliveries and maintain specifications for applicable materials/equipment.
	Lead supplier selection and pre-qualification process.
	Investigates and recommends sources of supply and purchases through contractual arrangements to affect cost savings.
	Obtains sources, quotes, generates, and processes requisitions, and maintains related records, logs, files, artwork as pertained to the purchasing department.
	Check all purchasing invoices for accuracy in billing. 
	Presents vendor cost comparison reports to upper management as requested. • Monitor and forecast upcoming levels of demand and track inventory levels.
	Other assigned tasks as necessary assigned by Management. 
	Responsible for following all safety regulations and complying with SOP's, EPA, OSHA, FDA, and cGMP's while performing duties.
	Maintain cleanliness and appearance of work area. 
	Well versed in analysis of spend data and cost models for continuous improvement.
	Maintain relationships with various functional groups across the organization including Manufacturing, Quality, Distribution, and Administration. 
	Other duties as assigned.


Requirements


	BSC / HND in any relevant field. However, a practical pharmaceutical manufacturing procurement experience is non-negotiable.
	5 - 6 years of procurement experience in the pharmaceutical industry Only( CANDIDATES WITHOUT PROCUREMENT PHARMACEUTICAL EXPERIENCE SHOULD NOT APPLY).
	Extensive knowledge of cGMP, FDA, and other pharmaceutical requirements.
	Experience in sourcing both pharmaceuticals, medical devices, and equipment.
	Experience of pharmaceutical supply chain systems in developing contexts and logistics.
	Successful experience in audit/assessments and the implementation of supply chain management improvements.
	Ability to identify trends within pharmaceuticals industry. 
	Ability to establish supplier specifications, measure conformance, and identify process improvement opportunities. 
	Knowledge of risk management, with the ability to identify and address anticipated issues. • Ability to work independently and troubleshoot challenges. 
	Must possess strong verbal, written, and oral communication skills.
	Must be able to work independently and with a team.
	Must possess problem-solving skills. 
	Recognize priorities and take action tomake productive use of time.


Salary
N400,000 – N500,000 / month.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their detailed CV to: transnationalzamiva21@yahoo.com or metusmetus73@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	You may also send your CV via whatsaap to: 09060194404
	Willingness to relocate to Mowe, Ogun State at the candidate's expense is also welcome
	Preference will be given to candidates living around Ikeja, Isheri, Ojodu-Berger, Alausa, Ojota, Ketu, Magodo, Omole, Magboro, Mowe and environs.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

	Assist in end-to-end recruitment processes (job postings, shortlisting, interviews, onboarding).

	Maintain and update employee records and HR databases accurately.

	A Relevant professional certificationisan advantage
	Advanced knowledge of HR analytics, HR software, Microsoft Office applications, especially Excel &amp; PowerPoint, is required
	Knowledge of HR technology is required
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.

Male HR / Admin Executive Job at Zamiva Transnational Services




        
    

        
        Zamiva Transnational Services is a full-fledged human capital development and management consultancy servicing company incorporated in August, 2021 with its corporate Head Office in Lagos, Nigeria. We are committed to ensuring quality value addition to our clients’ businesses spanning all sectors of the economy in the areas of HR consulting, outsourcing, talent acquisition, executive search, employee/reference background verification, payroll management, learning & development, renting, catering and janitorial services, etc., leveraging research, technology, a highly skilled and passionate human capital base, and our core values of Collaboration, Leadership, Integrity, Continuous Improvement, High Performance and Expertise.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



We are seasoned human resources professionals and management strategists with enviable corporate exposure and experience spanning various sectors of the economy, including banking, logistics & courier, engineering, manufacturing, pharmaceuticals, food & beverages, oil & gas, telecommunication, private security services, hospitality.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Male HR / Admin Executive
Job Location: Mowe, Ogun
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Assist in end-to-end recruitment processes (job postings, shortlisting, interviews, onboarding).
	ManageHR analytics to help management make informed data-driven decisions
	Maintain and update employee records and HR databases accurately.
	Support the administration of employee benefits, payroll inputs, and leave management.
	Help implement HR policies and procedures to ensure compliance with labor laws and company standards.
	Provide support in performance management activities, including appraisals and feedback processes.
	Address routine employee queries regarding HR policies, benefits, and procedures.
	Assist in organizing training sessions, employee engagement activities, and other HR-related events.
	Prepare HR-related reports as required (headcount, turnover, leave summaries, etc.).
	Support disciplinary and grievance processes under supervision.
	Perform other HR administrative duties as assigned
	Overtime management.


Requirements


	B.Sc in Human Resource Management/IRPM or allied disciplines
	A minimum of 4 years of experience as an HR /Admin Officer, preferably in a pharmaceutical manufacturing company, is very important
	A Relevant professional certificationisan advantage
	Advanced knowledge of HR analytics, HR software, Microsoft Office applications, especially Excel & PowerPoint, is required
	Knowledge of HR technology is required
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Knowledge of payroll administration is require
	Experience in overtime management is required.


Salary
N300,000 / month

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their detailed CV to: transnationalzamiva21@yahoo.com or metusmetus73@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note


	Candidates without manufacturing experience should not apply.
	Preference will be given to candidates living around Ikeja, Isheri, Ojodu-Berger, Alausa, Ojota, Ketu, Magodo, Omole, Magboro, Mowe and environs.
